title: upper fs does not support xattr, with --userns keepid, rhel 8.5 podman 3.3.1
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

On a fresh rhel 8.5 installation, when trying to launch containers using native overlayfs, with userns mapping  and user ID mapping (--userns keepid --user ${userid}), 

I receive the following error in the kernel logs,

` overlayfs: upper fs does not support xattr, falling back to index=off and metacopy=off.`

The container eventually starts after 10's of minutes. 
 
After the container starts from a fresh image. The container will start relatively quickly if the container is stopped and removed (--rm). However, using new images or running after a 'podman system reset'  will cause the issue again.

* Can workaround this issue by creating a local storage.conf and using fuse-overlayfs (restores previous behavior)
* I have not tested if the container fully operates as expected after it eventually launches with overlayfs
<!--

-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman system reset

2. Start a container with --userns keepid --user ${userid} // Where userid is a non-root user

3. Inspect /var/log/messages via `dmesg` or tail while starting a container

**Describe the results you received:**

kernel: overlayfs: upper fs does not support xattr, falling back to index=off and metacopy=off.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Container to start as normal

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Reproduceable

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.3.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body:
the container starts slowly because native overlay doesn't support idmapping of IDs, so the image must be cloned (and it takes time).
The error you are seeing is a test podman does to verify what features overlay support, and it should not affect your container.

What image are you using?

Does it happen even with small images (e.g., ubi, busybox)?
Also, please provide the full output of a `podman run` command that fails with the `--log-level=debug` flag added

@giuseppe   - Ok this make sense

I've tried a couple of container images
1) rhel minimal install + some packages ~2-3 GB
2) rhel minimal install + large application ~ 10GB

The smaller image has a 'faster' startup time ; Although extremely slow compared to fuse-overlay, clearly attributable to the clone

Are there any suggestions about improving this startup time?  Or are you aware if any kernel updates in the future will support idmapping? 

Hopefully there is a plan for enabling native overlay to perform better, but until then I am currently inclined to continue using fuse-overlay (unless you have some suggestions to minimize the impact).

sadly not yet.

We are waiting for idmapped mounts in the kernel to support overlay, which is not the case at the moment.

The issue is tracked here: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10374

I am closing this issue, since there is nothing we can do in Podman to address it.

The only alternative at the moment is to use fuse-overlayfs
